folder_role: Prompt template directory for the F1-F14 x CLI-executor matrix, providing standardized test prompts for cross-CLI feature validation.
architecture_observation: template-only - contains 14 markdown prompt templates that define standardized test payloads for matrix runner cells without any executable code or subdirectories.

public_entrypoints.name: F1-spec-folder.md
public_entrypoints.purpose: Matrix cell F1 prompt template for testing spec-folder workflow across CLI executors.

public_entrypoints.purpose: Matrix cell F2 prompt template for testing skill advisor and skill graph routing.

integration_notes: This folder is a core component of the matrix runner system that enables systematic cross-CLI testing of 14 key features (F1-F14) across 4 CLI executors (cli-codex, cli-gemini, cli-claude-code, cli-opencode). Each template defines a standardized prompt payload that is loaded by the matrix-manifest.json configuration and executed by run-matrix.ts through executor-specific adapters. The templates are referenced in the manifest's 'promptTemplate' field and are resolved relative to the matrix_runners directory. This system enables automated validation that each CLI executor can correctly handle the core system-spec-kit features, providing a matrix coverage of 56 cells (14 features × 4 executors) with selective applicability rules (e.g., F11 is marked as not applicable for cli-gemini). The templates follow a consistent pattern: they identify the matrix cell being executed, describe the inspection task, and specify the exact pass signal that the executor must output.
key_invariants: Each template file must follow the naming pattern F{N}-{feature-name}.md where N is 1-14
Each template must contain the exact pass signal 'MATRIX_CELL_PASS F{N}' where N matches the feature ID
Template filenames must match the 'promptTemplate' field references in matrix-manifest.json
Templates must be markdown files that define a clear inspection task for the target feature
Templates must not modify files - they are inspection-only test payloads
The templates directory must be located at matrix_runners/templates/ relative to the mcp_server root
Each template must be self-contained and not depend on other templates
Template content must be readable by the matrix runner's file loading mechanism
The total number of templates must match the number of distinct feature IDs in the matrix manifest (currently 14)
Templates must remain synchronized with the feature definitions in matrix-manifest.json
